raw_texts.Guidelines- Timesheets.docx: Timesheets What is a timesheet? - Timesheet is a tool we use to effectively ascertain the tasks that have been undertaken in the month and then bill the clients accordingly. It is not a tool to police your productivity, it is fuel to the organization, it is the only way we charge our clients and get paid. If we miss the timesheet,it has a direct impact on our finances. Why do we do the Timesheet? Why is it important to fill it regularly rather than at the end of month? It is important that we fill our timesheets regularly and not just at the end of the month. One of the biggest benefits is that if we do this regularly, recollecting how we spent time on activities is easier rather than thinking of it at the end of the month. This also means that we are able to achieve the following: - We get paid on time by our clients - late timesheets means late payment / missing pay cycle. One day delay can cause a 1 month delay in payments. - To check how we are doing in the month from a revenue perspective. - Predict the cash flow situation till date or for the week / month. - Predict client budget burn and plan according to their budgets. Why is logging it accurately important? We use our timesheets entries to raise invoices to our clients. We even send them an itemized billing that is prepared with your inputs. We take pride in this because very few companies really are able to give this break up at the time of raising the invoice. We want to make sure that with this we can: - Maintain Integrity - Do the right thing for our clients (i.e. neither undercharge, nor overcharge). - Get a good sense of our capacity. - Course correction if some people are getting burnt out. - Get our utilization (i.e. people on project vs people on beach) numbers right and plan our initiatives. Why 8 hours a day or 40 hours a week? Each individual is expected to put in 8 hours a day or 40 hours a week. Here is why: - This is a number which directly feeds into our planning for the entire year. - All our financial Investments (team outings, hiring plans, budget for various activities and initiatives) are based on this. - All our Budgeting for the year is based on this. - All our Salaries and appraisals are based on this. How does it affect if I miss filling the timesheet or put it under the wrong project? If we miss filling the timesheet or even a part of it, it has an impact on our revenue. If we miss recording 2 hours per week for example, we end up making a loss of 5% revenue for that person in that week. If we have put our time under the wrong project, then we will end up charging that client wrongly, which means we will end up not doing right by them. On the other hand, we will undercharge the actual client, which does not fare well too. Do more hours translate to more billing? - Yes and No. We may or may not get paid more for the extra hours. It depends on the client. Almost all the clients cap it to 40 hours generally. Sometimes we are able to bill if we have prior approval, many times we don’t. - Because our contracts are such that we commit to 40 hours a week. And clients base their budget based on this number. However this could be a good data point for us to figure if some is getting burnt out by working at 60 a week. What is included in 8 hours a day? Generally speaking these activities, and not limited to, constitute an 8 hour day. - Working on stories - Having project discussion - Project related Meetings - On-boarding or sharing context - Pairing or knowledge sharing for the project - Waiting for VPN or access (but there could be lot done without VPN) What is not included? - Lunch - Team outings - Personal work - Laptop breakdown - Commute to work - Meetups - Reading tech stack (unless investigating the current issues or approved by client to explore the tech) - Self learning - Open source contributions - Any Org wide contributions - Interviews .. basically any non-client activities. If there are too many such initiatives and it needs you to take some time off the project, then talk to the people team. How should it be filled? - Try to log a nearly accurate number of hours - Do not over optimize it. Eg. 5-10 mins chit chat, phone calls, breaks etc are fine but if that is frequent then put the right number. - Put a clear description which may include story number, brief description of tasks, meetings etc. Please do not put generic things like development, testing etc. unless the nature of work is completely ad hoc. In most of the projects it is not ad hoc. How do we view working on weekends? As a general rule, we discourage working on weekends. Weekends for you, your loved ones and to spend it on personal growth. Here is what we feel about working on weekends. - Don’t do it unless we plan for it or you think it is needed or if you are on support. Instead; - Spend time upskilling yourself. - Learn something new that will make you more productive on a project. - Explore new technology - Read books - Attend Meetups If there is production support or critical feature to take live then please inform your team for the same. I missed some hours this week, how do I cover the lost hours? - It is advisable to take leave if this is more than 3-4 hours - Keep your team informed about the lost hours - Cover the lost hours within the week itself than taking it to next week. Ensure that you  have things to work on in this duration. - Keep the team informed when you plan to cover and actually cover it. Like always in case of any confusion, please ask the people team and not assume.

1

 Introduction
We encourage Brightwave Labsrs to maintain a good work-life balance. Leaves are provided so
that we can take the deserved breaks for various reasons and sometimes, our favorite one,
for no reason at all! To help us take breaks whenever required, here are some guidelines.

Eligibility
● Brightwave Labsrs engaged as ‘employees’ are eligible to avail leaves.
●
●
●

Every Brightwave Labsr who is employed with Brightwave Labs is entitled to 24 annual leaves
in a calendar year (from January to December).
This basket of 24 annual leaves combines our casual leaves as well as sick leaves.
New Brightwave Labsrs will be entitled to annual leaves on a pro-rata basis depending
on the date they join.

Maternity and Paternity leaves
●
●

Maternity Leaves are as per the government policy and are a compliance that we all
follow.
Additionally, we can avail of Paternity Leave which is 1 week with full pay at
Brightwave Labs.

Kindly read the attached document in case there is a situation to avail of these special
leaves.

Bereavement Leaves
Please refer to the Bereavement Leave Guidelines here.

Compensatory off
Please refer to the Compensatory Off Guidelines here.

Unpaid Leaves
Every leave taken beyond the realm of the above leaves will be unpaid.
Loss of pay calculation - The loss of pay (as a result of taking unpaid leave) will be
calculated according to the number of working days in a month. It will have the following
formula:
(Monthly gross salary) x (No. of unpaid leaves taken)
○ LOP = ----------------------------------------------------------------------

2

 ○

(No. of working days in the month)
For e.g, if someone’s salary monthly gross salary is Rs.60,000 and they have
taken 4 days unpaid leave in March 2023 (which has 22 working days), then
their LOP = (60000 / 22) x 4 = Rs. 10,909 /-

Carry forward & Encashment of leaves
We have the option to carry forward a portion of unused annual leave from one calendar
year to the next. Here are the guidelines:
If there are more than 5 unused earned annual leaves we can choose to:
a. Carry forward upto maximum 5 days. It will be added to our new balance of
leaves.
b. Encash a maximum upto 20 days
c. A combination of a. and b.
Some points to keep in mind:
● If there are 5 unused earned annual leaves or less, we can choose to:
a. Carry forward all to the next calendar year. It will get added to our new
balance of leaves.
b. Encash all of them. The amount dues for this is credited in January.
The calculation for encashment will be as follows:

Encashment amount =

(Monthly gross salary) x (no. of leaves to be encashed)
---------------------------------------------------------(no. of working days in the encashment month)

Points to keep in mind
While we are encouraged to utilize all our leaves in the current calendar year to maintain a
healthy work-life balance, these flexible options allow us to manage our annual leave
balance according to our preferences.
● Half-day leave - A half-day leave will be counted if we have worked for at least 4
hours.
● Long leaves - It is advised to take not more than 15 annual leaves at a stretch
(excluding weekends). Every leave availed subsequently, which elongates this
stretch, will be considered unpaid. This is because such prolonged absences are
detrimental to the team’s productivity.

3

 What is our responsibility?
●

●

●
●

There are 3 places where we need to provide written notification of our leave.
○ Update the Leave Calendar
○ Update our Kimai with project @leave
○ Update #leave channel in Slack if it is a leave that is not planned.
Advanced notice - When we are applying for planned leaves, we need to inform our
team well in advance. Keep this formula in mind:
○ Advanced notice = (Planned leave duration) x 3
This means that if we are taking a 1 day planned leave, we need to inform our team
3 days in advance. If we are taking a 1 week planned leave, we should inform our
team 3 weeks in advance… and so on. We are requested to follow this guideline to
avoid our leaves being unapproved.
We can also use /timesheet help command on slack to get help on how to add
leaves and timesheet entries for our leaves.
We will follow the same for availing of any optional holiday as well.

Where can I see my leaves?
Leave balances, historic leave data and optional Holidays selected can be referred to in the
Leaves sheet.

Point of Contact
People Team - peopleteam@brightwavelabs.com

1

 Introduction
Festivals and celebrations are an important part of our culture. As Brightwave Labsrs are
from diverse ethnicities, we take pride in offering a comprehensive list of Holidays
where Brightwave Labsrs can enjoy the festivals and celebrations as they wish. Our list of
Holidays is divided into two broad categories; Fixed and Optional.

Eligibility
1.​ Any Brightwave Labsr working full time is eligible for optional holidays.
2.​ To avail of both Optional Holidays, we must have been with Brightwave Labs for
more than 6 calendar months during the year.
3.​ If we join or leave mid-year without completing 6 months, we are entitled to
only 1 Optional Holiday for that year. If 2 Optional Holidays are already taken,
and we leave before 6 months, 1 leave will be deducted from our Full and
Final settlement.

Guidelines for Fixed Holidays
●​ The below days are Fixed Holidays in Brightwave Labs for the year 2026..
●​ If, because of delivery commitments, we are working on any of the above
Holidays, then we will be eligible for an additional annual leave(s) in lieu
accordingly.
●​ Even if we are working at the client location, our leaves and public holidays
are still governed as per the guidelines at Brightwave Labs.

Guidelines for Optional Holidays
●​ Optional Holidays can be chosen according to an individual's preference.
●​ Once an Optional Holiday is taken, requests for changes are not permissible.
For instance, if an Optional Holiday for January 14th is used, it cannot later be
switched to August 11th.
●​ Uniform Guidelines: Even when working at a client's location, leave and public
holiday policies align with Brightwave Labs's guidelines.
●​ These guidelines ensure that employees can choose Optional Holidays as per
their preferences while adhering to the company's operational requirements
and fairness standards.

 What is our responsibility?
●​ Similar to planned leaves, advance notice is required to take an Optional
Holiday. This ensures smooth coordination with our client and team. We
should inform about us availing an optional holiday 3 days in advance.
●​ Information regarding Optional Holidays will be communicated through the
Leave Channel via the Leave Calendar. We can also refer to the Leave Policy
for available Optional Holidays. Personal reminders for Optional Holiday
utilization will not be sent.
●​ We need to add our optional holiday well in advance to our Kimai/Timesheets
to avoid any last-minute confusion/hassle.

Point of Contact

 Introduction:
The loss of a dear one is a difficult time. Brightwave Labs would like to extend the support which is
needed by having a provision to take bereavement leave.

Eligibility:
●
●
●

Any Brightwave Labsr who is full-time is eligible to avail leaves.
A fully paid bereavement leave of 5 working days to support our families.
Family members who are included for availing the Bereavement Leave are:
○ Immediate family - Grandparents, Parents, Siblings, Spouse, Children
○ In-laws - Parents in law, Siblings in law

What is my responsibility?
Please follow the Annual Leave document to understand our responsibilities.

Where can I see my leaves?
Leave balances, historic leave data and optional Holidays selected can be referred to in the
Leaves sheet.

Point of Contact:
People Team - peopleteam@brightwavelabs.com
